original_hypothesis.title: TITN Insolvency via Unlimited TGT Deposits – No Cap Enforcement
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: onTokenTransfer accepts unlimited TGT deposits and allocates proportional TITN via quoteTitn without checking whether cumulative allocated TITN exceeds the available TITN_ARB (173.7M). If total TGT deposited exceeds TGT_TO_EXCHANGE (579M), totalTitnClaimable exceeds TITN_ARB, rendering the contract insolvent. TGT max supply is ~1B (TGT_TO_EXCHANGE is 57.9% of it), so depositing the full supply would over-commit TITN by ~72%. Early claimers can drain the contract via claimTitn, leaving late claimers unable to claim.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a fundamental solvency defect: the contract mints obligations (claimable TITN accounting entries) without verifying they can be backed by actual TITN balance. Any TGT holder can deposit, making this exploitable by any on-chain actor. The resulting bank-run dynamic economically rewards early detection and claiming.

proof_of_signal.pattern: onTokenTransfer increments claimableTitnPerUser and totalTitnClaimable by quoteTitn(amount) without any check that totalTitnClaimable + totalTitnClaimed <= TITN_ARB or titn.balanceOf(this)

preconditions: Total TGT deposited via onTokenTransfer exceeds TGT_TO_EXCHANGE (579M TGT)
lockedStatus != Locked and launchTime > 0 and within 360 day window
TGT total supply is ~1B (confirmed by constant comment: '57.9% of MAX_TGT')

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: quoteTitn: (tgtAmount * TITN_ARB) / TGT_TO_EXCHANGE — proportional per-deposit with no cumulative cap
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Any TGT holder deposits TGT; if aggregate deposits exceed 579M TGT, totalTitnClaimable > 173.7M TITN but only 173.7M TITN exists in contract
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Early claimers who detect insolvency can race to call claimTitn() and drain available TITN. If 1B TGT deposited in first 90d, ~300M TITN allocated but only 173.7M available. First ~58% of claimers succeed, rest get nothing.

economic_questions: What is the actual circulating TGT supply on Arbitrum?
At what TGT deposit threshold does insolvency begin?
How quickly could a bank run drain the contract once insolvency is detected?
validation_requirements: Simulate cumulative deposits exceeding TGT_TO_EXCHANGE and verify totalTitnClaimable > TITN_ARB
Verify claimTitn reverts for later claimers due to insufficient TITN balance
Test with realistic deposit patterns based on TGT supply distribution

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: Any amount of TGT (contributes to over-allocation; exploitation requires being an early claimer)
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_execution_cost: Gas for claimTitn call (~0.001 ETH on Arbitrum)
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Up to 100% of depositor's expected TITN if they claim first; later claimers lose 100%
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: Depends on position in claim queue; first ~58% of total claims succeed at full value
preliminary_attack_economics.attack_duration: Over multiple blocks as deposits accumulate; bank run resolves in minutes once detected

market_conditions_analysis.liquidity_requirements: No special liquidity needed; any TGT holders depositing creates the condition
market_conditions_analysis.optimal_timing: During first 90 days when conversion rate is highest, maximizing over-allocation per TGT deposited
market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Protocol-breaking: complete loss of trust, TITN value collapse

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All TGT depositors who claim after TITN balance is exhausted
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: 100% loss of expected TITN for late claimers

investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: If 1B TGT deposited in 90d: 1B * 173.7M/579M = ~300M TITN allocated vs 173.7M available

validation_result.mechanism: Unbounded TITN allocation without cumulative cap
validation_result.impact_summary: If total TGT deposited via onTokenTransfer exceeds TGT_TO_EXCHANGE (579M), totalTitnClaimable exceeds TITN_ARB (173.7M TITN actually held), rendering the contract insolvent and causing later claimers to fail.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The onTokenTransfer function (MergeTgt.sol:67-84) accepts any amount of TGT and computes a proportional TITN allocation via quoteTitn, then adds it to totalTitnClaimable without any check that the cumulative total stays within TITN_ARB. The quoteTitn formula `(tgtAmount * TITN_ARB) / TGT_TO_EXCHANGE` is a per-deposit linear proportion: if exactly TGT_TO_EXCHANGE total TGT is deposited, totalTitnClaimable equals exactly TITN_ARB. But since TGT has a max supply of ~1B (579M is 57.9% per the comment), depositing more than 579M TGT will push totalTitnClaimable above 173.7M TITN. Since the contract only holds 173.7M TITN (enforced by the deposit function at line 48-50), safeTransfer in claimTitn will revert for users who try to claim after the balance is exhausted. This is a textbook insolvency defect—obligations are created without verifying they can be fulfilled.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: Lines 83-84 of MergeTgt.sol increment claimableTitnPerUser and totalTitnClaimable with no upper bound check. Lines 48-50 enforce the deposit amount equals TITN_ARB exactly. Lines 135-141 compute a per-deposit proportional amount that, when summed over all deposits exceeding TGT_TO_EXCHANGE, yields a total exceeding TITN_ARB. There is no code anywhere in onTokenTransfer that checks `totalTitnClaimable + titnOut <= TITN_ARB` or `totalTitnClaimable <= titn.balanceOf(address(this))`.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: No check that totalTitnClaimable + titnOut <= TITN_ARB (or titn.balanceOf(this)). Each deposit independently computes a proportional allocation; cumulative deposits exceeding TGT_TO_EXCHANGE cause totalTitnClaimable to exceed TITN_ARB, creating unbacked obligations.

The formula computes TITN output per deposit proportionally. It is correct for any single deposit but has no awareness of cumulative state. If sum of all tgtAmount inputs exceeds TGT_TO_EXCHANGE, sum of all outputs exceeds TITN_ARB.
safeTransfer on line 108 will revert when contract TITN balance is exhausted, causing later claimers to be unable to claim their legitimately credited amounts.

calling_sequence: 1. Multiple TGT holders call TGT.transferAndCall(MergeTgt, amount) which triggers onTokenTransfer
2. onTokenTransfer validates msg.sender == tgt, lockedStatus, launchTime, amount, and time window
3. quoteTitn(amount) computes titnOut = (amount * 173.7M) / 579M for first 90 days
4. claimableTitnPerUser[from] += titnOut and totalTitnClaimable += titnOut with NO cap check
5. After cumulative TGT > 579M, totalTitnClaimable > 173.7M (the actual TITN balance)
6. Early users call claimTitn() successfully, draining TITN balance
7. Later users call claimTitn() but titn.safeTransfer reverts due to insufficient balance
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The critical path is onTokenTransfer → quoteTitn → state update (lines 83-84). There is no guard between the quoteTitn calculation and the state update that checks totalTitnClaimable against any cap. The contract relies entirely on the assumption that total TGT deposited won't exceed TGT_TO_EXCHANGE, but this assumption is unenforced. TGT max supply (~1B) exceeds TGT_TO_EXCHANGE (579M), so the precondition is satisfiable. Once totalTitnClaimable > titn.balanceOf(this), the contract is insolvent.

deployment_assumptions_checked: TITN deposit amount is exactly TITN_ARB (173.7M)
config_validation.deployment_evidence: arbitrumSetup.ts confirms TITN_DEPOSIT_AMOUNT = '173700000000000000000000000' (173.7M TITN). The deposit() function at line 48-50 enforces amount == TITN_ARB. The contract comment confirms TGT_TO_EXCHANGE is 57.9% of MAX_TGT, meaning MAX_TGT ≈ 1B > 579M.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: Scenario: During the first 90 days, TGT holders collectively deposit 800M TGT (TGT total supply is ~1B). At the full rate, totalTitnClaimable = 800M * 173.7M / 579M ≈ 240M TITN. The contract only holds 173.7M TITN. If all users try to claim, only the first ~72% (173.7M/240M) worth of claims succeed. Later claimers' safeTransfer calls revert. This creates a bank-run dynamic: once the insolvency threshold is crossed, rational actors race to claim first. Additionally, withdrawRemainingTitn after 360 days would attempt `unclaimedTitn = remainingTitnAfter1Year - initialTotalClaimable` which underflows since the remaining balance < initialTotalClaimable, permanently blocking post-year claims.
validation_result.impact_assessment: CRITICAL. If TGT deposits exceed 579M (which is feasible given ~1B total TGT supply), the contract becomes insolvent. Early claimers drain the TITN pool; late claimers lose 100% of their expected TITN. The withdrawRemainingTitn function is additionally broken by underflow. The entire 173.7M TITN allocation is at risk.
recommended_mitigations: Add a cumulative cap check in onTokenTransfer: require(totalTitnClaimable + titnOut <= TITN_ARB, 'Cap exceeded')
Alternatively, track total TGT deposited and reject deposits that would push total beyond TGT_TO_EXCHANGE
Add a minimum output check: require(titnOut > 0, 'Zero output') to prevent boundary deposits yielding nothing
Consider adding a require(titnOut > minOutput) parameter for slippage protection
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy MergeTgt with mock TGT and TITN tokens. 2. Owner deposits 173.7M TITN. 3. Set launch time and unlock. 4. Have multiple accounts deposit a total of 600M TGT (above 579M cap) within 90 days. 5. Verify totalTitnClaimable > 173.7M TITN. 6. Have early users claimTitn successfully. 7. Verify later users' claimTitn reverts with insufficient balance. 8. Advance time past 360 days and verify withdrawRemainingTitn reverts with underflow.
uncertainties: Actual circulating TGT supply on Arbitrum at launch - if less than 579M TGT is accessible on Arbitrum, the threshold may not be reached in practice
Whether the owner would manually lock deposits (setLockedStatus(Locked)) before the cap is reached - this is an operational mitigation but not a code-level fix
additional_testing_needed: Determine actual TGT circulating supply on Arbitrum to assess practical exploitability
Test exact boundary behavior at totalTitnClaimable == TITN_ARB
Fuzz test with various deposit patterns to identify minimum TGT needed to trigger insolvency
Test withdrawRemainingTitn underflow when totalTitnClaimable > contract TITN balance
